notes_md:
**Chú** = your father’s younger brother (uncle on your dad’s side).  

It’s a noun: say **một người chú**, **hai người chú**; in family talk, you often hear **chú + name**.  

Very common in speech: you use **chú** to address a man older than you but younger than your parents (like “uncle” in English, but broader).  

It can also be a pronoun: an older man may call himself **chú** when talking to a child.  

Compare: **bác** (parent’s older sibling) — older than your parent, while **chú** is younger than your father.
notes: Chú = your father’s younger brother (uncle on your dad’s side). It’s a noun: say một người chú, hai người chú; in family talk, you often hear chú + name. Very common in speech: you use chú to address a man older than you but younger than your parents (like “uncle” in English, but broader). It can also be a pronoun: an older man may call himself chú when talking to a child. Compare: bác (parent’s older sibling) — older than your parent, while chú is younger than your father.

definitions.vi: Em trai của bố hoặc cách gọi người đàn ông trung niên.
definitions.en: Father's younger brother or a way to address middle-aged men.
definitions.es: Hermano menor của padre o una forma de dirigirse a hombres de mediana edad.

exampleSentences: Em thường đi chơi với chú vào cuối tuần khi rảnh.
Chú của anh sống ở thành phố khác nên ít khi gặp.
Hôm qua, chú tặng cho em một cuốn sách rất hay.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I often hang out with my uncle on weekends when I am free.
My uncle lives in another city so I rarely see him.
Yesterday, my uncle gave me a very interesting book.

content.sentence: ___ em vừa mới đi làm về.

translation.en: My uncle just got home from work.
